title: False Liquidity And Pull Behavior
focus_questions: Did the displayed size stay and produce outcome, or pull before pressure?
Was the level real structure or only visual bait?

summary.thesis: Displayed size is false-liquidity risk when it repeatedly attracts attention but pulls, relocates, or fails to produce real defense when price approaches.
summary.operator_translation: Do not trust size for control until lifecycle plus outcome proves it is real.
summary.ai_usage_note: Use this card when visible size appears important but keeps failing to create real support or resistance behavior.
summary.ai_brief: Environment: displayed size near an active decision zone. Required evidence: pull, relocation, or no real price outcome after the size appears. Do not trade: from brightness alone or before lifecycle evidence is clear. Real trigger: repeated pull-before-touch or displayed size without defense. Failure point: the size starts staying, reloading, and producing real outcome.

when_relevant: Displayed size appears near an active level.
The operator is tempted to infer support or resistance from visibility alone.
Order lifecycle and outcome still need to be judged.
required_evidence: the size can be observed pulling, relocating, or failing to produce defense
price outcome after the displayed size appears can be reviewed
confirmation_signals: the displayed order pulls or relocates before meaningful interaction
price does not react the way a truly defended level should react
repeated flashes of size fail to create persistent support or resistance behavior
invalidation_signals: the size stays and repeatedly produces real defense
same-price replenishment begins proving true control instead of bait
price outcome starts matching a real defended level
no_trade_conditions: Do not lean on displayed size alone without lifecycle evidence.
Do not call a level defended if the size repeatedly pulls before pressure arrives.
Do not fade price only because a large wall appears if that wall has not produced outcome before.

entry_archetypes.name: post-pull continuation
entry_archetypes.description: Trade after the supposed wall or bid pulls and price continues through the area with no real defense.
entry_archetypes.risk_note: Avoid if the level still has unresolved counterflow and no clean outcome yet.

entry_archetypes.name: second failed visual defense
entry_archetypes.description: Trade after repeated visual size fails again to produce the expected defense.
entry_archetypes.risk_note: Avoid if the second test actually begins to hold.

management_notes: This card is best used with live-depth and trapped-order review.
Repeated visual bait without outcome is stronger evidence than one isolated flash.
If size starts staying and producing outcome, hand the read off to a real defense card.
review_questions: What actually happened to the visible size when price got close?
Did the operator trade the size itself or the outcome it produced?
Was the supposed wall or bid only visual bait that disappeared under pressure?
